pains.summary: No visibility into which campaigns are actually driving conversions vs. just active in system
pains.quote: there are a bunch of campaigns that have never been marked inactive that is being captured in every single quarter
pains.speaker: Ram Arunachalam

pains.summary: Channel classification confusion causing paid social to appear with zero spend
pains.quote: paid social is zero, Facebook is zero, paid social has nine campaigns, and LinkedIn has nine campaigns. So is that like the duplication?
pains.speaker: Ram Arunachalam

pains.summary: Facebook spend data showing contributions after spend was turned off, indicating attribution decay issues
pains.quote: well we you know I realize you know with ad stock and all that there's that decay but it's it I'd have to check but my guess is it stopped uh somewhere around April we turned off Facebook that's probably a couple only a couple weeks of spend there all of for most of q2 all of q3 and all but the last couple of weeks of q4 there was no facebook spent
pains.speaker: Harry Hawk

pains.summary: Sales efficiency metric appearing when nothing is for sale yet
pains.quote: Which like there's no price. There's nothing for sale yet. Next week, there'll be something for sale. again sales efficiency like shouldn't be there in my mind
pains.speaker: Harry Hawk

pains.summary: Campaigns with zero dollars spent still appearing in reports, causing confusion about where spend is being allocated
pains.quote: in q4 i'm looking at like a table uh where you have four campaigns in facebook uh seven campaigns in linkedin and four campaigns in paid social with zero dollars spent so we just need to dive into what those four campaigns are and maybe rename
pains.speaker: Ram Arunachalam

pains.summary: Dashboard filters failing to load with 'all time' timeframe, causing data fetch failures
pains.quote: Every time I put all time, this like freaks out and does not complete.
pains.speaker: Francisco Oller Garcia

pains.summary: Conversion values incorrectly set too high ($5 instead of 50 cents) due to hardcoded values
pains.quote: It should be 50 cents. And it's doing like $5. And we're sending it at 50 cents on our side and I hardcoded it here.
pains.speaker: Harry Hawk

pains.summary: Purchase event firing on every agent run instead of actual purchases, causing inflated conversion numbers
pains.quote: 35,000 purchases in the last seven days. We're doing great. Oh, it looks like a lot of these are. Okay, these might be runs then. Yeah. These are all individual runs.
pains.speaker: Adam Jordan

pains.summary: Mixpanel used as primary analytics but not connected to ad optimization, creating signal gap
pains.quote: We've always just used mixed panel as the, you know, we're driving down the road, but we're looking at mixed panel. The conversions here really just, you know, the revsure ones really just get the signal to Google, you know, wouldn't optimize for it.
pains.speaker: Harry Hawk

pains.summary: Need to clean up custom events and remove conversion actions in Google
pains.quote:
Adam Jordan | 3:00:10 AM -->
 they  completed  the  run  actions,  yeah,  if  they  completed  the  run  action  three  times  on  that  one  page  then  okay,  it's  that  one  but  Yeah,  okay,  I  do  have  to  hop  for  another  meeting  this  was  very  helpful.  Thank  you  so  much  I'll  clean  up  those  custom  events  and  remove  the  conversion  actions  that  we  talked  about  in  Google.
pains.speaker: Adam Jordan

contrarians.claim: Agent marketplace should be Switzerland - neutral platform that doesn't compete with partners
contrarians.why_unusual: Most platforms compete with their ecosystem; Agent.AI explicitly avoids this
contrarians.quote: as opposed to sort of, you know, you know, being competitive, we're really trying to be Switzerland here and, and try to be a partnership opportunity for everybody
contrarians.speaker: Dave Rouse

contrarians.claim: Each individual will hire agents on their own behalf, not just companies buying for teams
contrarians.why_unusual: Contradicts typical B2B SaaS seat-based model; predicts consumer-like individual agent adoption
contrarians.quote: each individual hiring agents on their own work workload behalf
contrarians.speaker: Dave Rouse

contrarians.claim: LinkedIn scraping is 'pretty normal thing right now everyone scrapes LinkedIn'
contrarians.why_unusual: Openly acknowledges controversial practice that vendors typically deny or obfuscate
contrarians.quote: I think it's a pretty like normal thing right now everyone scrapes LinkedIn for yeah
contrarians.speaker: Tejas

contrarians.claim: Sending MAWAU signals every single day for active users rather than just once, creating overlapping daily conversion signals
contrarians.why_unusual: Standard practice sends conversion events once per user journey; daily restatement of the same user as 'new conversion' intentionally pollutes attribution with repeated signals
contrarians.quote: the middle one, the Mauau, is sent every day. If you're Mauau today, tomorrow, every day that you are, it gets sent. The new is only during the time that you're new. And if it has a drop, it never starts again. And then the retained can start and stop.
contrarians.speaker: Harry Hawk

contrarians.claim: Treating YouTube subscriber acquisition (60 cents each) as comparable metric to B2B SaaS conversion optimization
contrarians.why_unusual: Consumer social subscriber growth metrics rarely inform B2B SaaS paid acquisition strategy; conflating these suggests unconventional full-funnel thinking
contrarians.quote: The most successful campaign in my opinion recently we did a speed run to a hundred thousand subscribers on YouTube Mm-hmm, and it was getting like 60 cents a subscription or something like that originally even cheaper than that
contrarians.speaker: Harry Hawk

revsure_problems.problem: Channel classification showing campaigns with zero spend as 'paid social'
revsure_problems.quote: paid social is zero, Facebook is zero, paid social has nine campaigns, and LinkedIn has nine campaigns. So is that like the duplication?
revsure_problems.speaker: Ram Arunachalam

revsure_problems.problem: Backend events (agent run completed) not capturing in RevSure
revsure_problems.quote: back-end events initialized and completed are not getting captured
revsure_problems.speaker: Sai Raju

revsure_problems.problem: Saved view filters don't persist - lead member and customer filters turn back on
revsure_problems.quote: I turn off lead member and customer and apply and save and at some future point I come back and they're all turned on again
revsure_problems.speaker: Harry Hawk

revsure_problems.problem: Client confusion about difference between 'paid search' and 'Google' channels in MMX
revsure_problems.quote: we're seeing what are clearly hubspot um types so we should do the same filtering and just confirm and then those should get part of be part of email newsletter
revsure_problems.speaker: Harry Hawk

revsure_problems.problem: Dashboard overview not loading, requiring page refresh
revsure_problems.quote: weird that the overview is not loading
revsure_problems.speaker: Harry Hawk

revsure_problems.problem: Filter 'all time' timeframe causes data fetch failures and incomplete loading
revsure_problems.quote: Every time I put all time, this like freaks out and does not complete.
revsure_problems.speaker: Francisco Oller Garcia

revsure_problems.problem: Smart filters failing to fetch data with 'fail to fetch' error message
revsure_problems.quote: see the horrible message that says fail to fetch data. Yeah,
revsure_problems.speaker: Harry Hawk

revsure_problems.problem: Campaign name dimension not available as default, requiring custom configuration
revsure_problems.quote: some of these aren't even defaults on the dimensions. There's like two or three that are.
revsure_problems.speaker: Francisco Oller Garcia

revsure_problems.problem: Facebook CAPI implementation has problems being fixed, blocking offline conversion setup
revsure_problems.quote: we're not doing anything for meta, where there was a problem with the CAPI implementation, and we're in the process of fixing that.
revsure_problems.speaker: Harry Hawk

best_about_revsure.summary: RevSure's data model and semantic layer praised by Agent.AI product leader
best_about_revsure.quote: great product agent.ai, so congratulations. It's very inspirational what you guys are doing. So, and we take a lot of inspiration from how you guys have thought through the framework of agent building
best_about_revsure.speaker: Deepinder Singh Dhingra

best_about_revsure.summary: John's deployment tracking visualization praised as 'brilliant' and 'beautiful'
best_about_revsure.quote: this he created this bottom part here so it's showing the key pieces that they purchased and the color as you go left to right is showing how much of that has been deployed very beautiful
best_about_revsure.speaker: Harry Hawk

best_about_revsure.summary: RevSure enables complex multi-step flow tracking for premium agent conversions
best_about_revsure.quote: If it has flow in it, it's a multi-step piece. and I'll, I'll walk you through each of these.
best_about_revsure.speaker: Harry Hawk

immediate.win: Organic vs paid traffic report built to understand what's driving agent runs
immediate.quote: the goal of the report for Harry was to understand what's driving people to our site to do the agent runs, right? Is it from organic channels or is it from a paid channel
immediate.speaker: Francisco Oller Garcia

immediate.win: LinkedIn enrichment added to contact enrichment flow for personalized emails
immediate.quote: in the contact enrichment flow, we're adding LinkedIn as a source as an enrichment source
immediate.speaker: Tejas

immediate.win: Deep funnel optimization setup for agent run completed events
immediate.quote: we should create um a dfo um for agent run completed
immediate.speaker: Harry Hawk

immediate.win: Identified and fixed domain allowlist issue blocking landing page pixel tracking
immediate.quote: So yes, pro dot agent AI So if you should see on your end that I've added agent AI and pro dot agent AI
immediate.speaker: Harry Hawk

immediate.win: Identified agent ran completed as best signal for free user engagement on Meta
immediate.quote: AgentRan. Because that's equivalent of becoming a member and at least running one agent
immediate.speaker: Harry Hawk

long_term.win: Build agent on Agent.AI platform using RevSure data for account intelligence
long_term.quote: what could be a shore agent on top of agent.ai that we could offer. Yeah. That could be connected to the underlying HubSpot platform and HubSpot data
long_term.speaker: Deepinder Singh Dhingra

long_term.win: Use RevSure as central data lake with Snowflake for product analytics
long_term.quote: the intent is basically that we can stop set trying to sink everything, you know, in a bunch of like messy sinks and actually like push more directly and clean it up there
long_term.speaker: Max Freiert

long_term.win: Prove MMX value to prevent churn and secure renewal beyond current contract
long_term.quote: we need to for lack of a better word seduce these folks into using revsure right because they could just say oh you know we're gonna do these other things
long_term.speaker: Harry Hawk

before_state.pain_summary: Data scattered across multiple systems with no unified view; discrepancies between Mixpanel, HubSpot, and ad platforms impossible to debug; no clear attribution for agent runs vs marketing site visits

after_state.with_revsure: Centralizing GTM data in RevSure with semantic data model, building attribution reports for organic vs paid, configuring WAMU metrics, and attempting deep funnel optimization - but facing UI bugs, writeback misconfigurations, and political resistance from marketing leadership
wins_realized: organic vs paid traffic comparison report
domain allowlist fixed for landing page tracking
agent ran completed identified as key Meta signal
open_issues: Google Ads writeback to correct account
premium user HubSpot proxy not created
